text: The country is networked with 200 micron fiberoptic fibers, to move data around the country. I used to make that some years ago. The space station used a smaller micron, I want to say it was 120 micron though it was some years ago. Slot machines use 200 micron. Im not familiar with photonics though sounds fascinating. There are interesting properties regarding nanometers, what light is measured in, and a reverse scale, tHz. Once refined, think of them as reverse scales. 540nm = 540tHz. 360nm = 720tHz. Their balances do some interesting things and I feel will be the future of data processing though not how one may think. Happy to explain if there is interest though I’ll warn you, cryptographers can’t keep up and the average person has no interest. Personally, I think I could teach it to the average eight year old, people are just set in there ways. Once quantum computing becomes a threat, there will be no choice other then to accept it. Personally, Im not holding my breath…

text:
$CRDO - CREDO IS BUILDING THE WHOLE AI-DATACENTER CONNECTIVITY STACK, AND IT RIPPED 7% TODAY

Credo's fiscal-Q4 revenue hit $437M, up 157% year over year, as hyperscalers standardize on its ZeroFlap active electrical cables for power-efficient in-rack interconnect. The DustPhotonics acquisition extends Credo from copper AECs into silicon photonics across 800G, 1.6T and 3.2T, and a new Rebellions tie-up drops its cables straight into RebelPOD AI clusters.

The mechanism is vertical integration of the unglamorous layer AI cannot scale without: as GPU clusters grow, the bottleneck moves from compute to moving bits between racks at low power — and Credo now sells both the copper and the optics for that hop.

A nice relief rally for Quantum today.  

$IONQ the most impressive mover of the day, the stock is likely to be totally re-rated upon successful $SKYT acquisition

$QNT couldn't hold $60 on the session on day 3 in the public markets, but it's not too far off it's IPO valuation 

$INFQ undervalued and under-appreciated. Accumulation zone. 

$XNDU still one of the best plays to get exposure to  photonics, quantum computing and PennyLane all in one company.

text:
🚨The AI chips are useless without it. 

A GPU is just expensive silicon until you can move data between them at the speed AI demands and that connection layer is becoming the real bottleneck.

That's the $CRDO thesis. And I've been long for a while.

$CRDO built its name on copper connectivity -> the SerDes, DSPs, and active electrical cables that move data between AI chips. The one open question was always what happens when the industry shifts from copper to optical. With the DustPhotonics acquisition, Credo brought silicon photonics in-house and now owns the full connectivity stack: SerDes → DSP → active cables → optical transceivers → silicon photonics, spanning 800G to a 3.2T roadmap. Electrical and optical, end to end.

That's the moat. When Jensen says the future of AI depends on connectivity, this is the layer he means and $CRDO is the pure-play that owns all of it.

The fundamentals: FY26 revenue more than tripled to $1.34B, non-GAAP net income rose 5x to $662M, ~68% gross margins, $1.4B cash and no debt.

$CRDO - run the forward math*👇

→ Revenue CAGR (FY26–FY28): ~61%
→ PEG: ~0.52 -> Re-Rate ✨
→ EV/Sales: ~17x FY27 → ~11x FY28
→ Forward P/E: ~65x trailing → ~42x FY27 → ~27x FY28

A company compounding revenue at ~61% with a PEG of ~0.5.

The multiple isn't the story. The growth is. Watch the "expensive" 65x compress as earnings catch up to 42x, then 27x.

“Expensive” on today's numbers. Cheap on where it's going + re-rate.

Everyone's buying the chips.

The chips are useless if you can't connect them.

$CRDO owns the connection. 👀

*Estimated from projections

raw: {"__typename": "Tweet", "card": {"legacy": {"binding_values": [{"key": "unified_card", "value": {"string_value": "{\"component_objects\":{\"details_1\":{\"type\":\"grok_share\",\"data\":{\"profile_user\":{\"id_str\":\"1235608350861045761\",\"profile_image_url_https\":\"https:\\/\\/pbs.twimg.com\\/profile_images\\/1826080471261872128\\/qAVKZ2MG.jpg\",\"name\":\"Cyberpunk Sense \\ud83d\\udc51\",\"screen_name\":\"napoleon21st\",\"verified\":false,\"is_blue_verified\":true},\"grok_user\":{\"id_str\":\"1720665183188922368\",\"profile_image_url_https\":\"https:\\/\\/pbs.twimg.com\\/profile_images\\/2057885623374290944\\/lmj0pawP.jpg\",\"name\":\"Grok\",\"screen_name\":\"grok\",\"highlighted_label\":{\"description\":\"xAI\",\"badge\":{\"url\":\"https:\\/\\/pbs.twimg.com\\/profile_images\\/1769430779845611520\\/lIgjSJGU_bigger.jpg\"},\"url\":{\"urlType\":\"DeepLink\",\"url\":\"https:\\/\\/twitter.com\\/xai\"},\"userLabelType\":\"BusinessLabel\",\"userLabelDisplayType\":\"Badge\"},\"verified\":false,\"is_blue_verified\":true,\"verified_type\":\"Business\"},\"id\":\"d8d7b8e1888642bd809f26377fcae693\",\"conversation_preview\":[{\"message\":\"Does Sivers only benefit from CPO or also from and NPO and LPO\",\"sender\":\"USER\",\"grokMode\":\"NORMAL\",\"mediaUrls\":[]},{\"message\":\"Sivers Semiconductors (Sivers Photonics) benefits from CPO, NPO, and LPO\\u2014not just CPO.\\nSivers specializes in high-performance III-V laser arrays (especially InP-based DFB laser arrays) that act as the light sources (often called external laser sources or ELS) for silicon photonics optical engines in next-generation AI data center interconnects. These lasers are a foundational component across multiple optical architectures that are evolving to handle higher bandwidth, lower power, and greater scale than traditional pluggable transceivers.\\nHere\\u2019s a quick breakdown of the acronyms in this context (all are key technologies for AI-scale data centers):\\nCPO (Co-Packaged Optics): Optics integrated directly into the same package as the switch ASIC or GPU for minimal electrical distance, highest density, and lowest power\\/latency. This is the most advanced \\u201cscale-up\\u201d approach.\\nNPO (Near-Packaged Optics): A practical middle-ground between traditional pluggables and full CPO. Optics are placed very close to the ASIC (on the same board\\/substrate but socketable\\/separable) via short electrical traces. It delivers big power and performance gains with better serviceability than CPO.\\nLPO (Linear Pluggable Optics \\/ Linear-drive Pluggable Optics): An evolutionary step for pluggable modules (still front-panel swappable) that removes the power-hungry DSP chip and uses direct linear drive from the SerDes. It cuts power and cost while keeping compatibility with existing infrastructure.\\nSivers\\u2019 lasers are used (or designed for use) in all three:\\nExplicit confirmation for CPO and LPO: In a June 2026 strategic collaboration with GlobalFoundries, Sivers\\u2019 laser arrays are integrated into GF\\u2019s silicon photonics platform and SCALE\\u2122 CPO optical engine. The announcement states this supports \\u201ca range of optical connectivity architectures, including co-packaged optics (CPO), linear pluggable optics (LPO), and other emerging data center interconnect solutions.\\u201d\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"bb11c6\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e0\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nCPO focus in other partnerships: Sivers has multiple CPO-specific deals (e.g., with O-Net Technologies and Enablence for external light source modules used in AI switches and NICs).\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"e46912\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e7\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nNPO coverage: NPO is one of the main \\u201cemerging\\u201d architectures alongside CPO and LPO (as discussed in industry panels and analyses, including Lumentum\\u2019s own OFC 2026 references to CPO\\/NPO\\/xPO). Sivers\\u2019 lasers power silicon photonics engines used in NPO designs, just as they do for CPO. The GF partnership\\u2019s broad language (\\u201cother emerging\\u201d) and Sivers\\u2019 positioning in the overall AI optics supply chain extend to NPO.\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"ed69f2\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e26\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nIn short, while Sivers is often highlighted for the CPO opportunity (a big AI growth driver), its laser technology is not limited to one architecture. It supplies critical light sources across the full spectrum of high-speed optical interconnects that hyperscalers and non-NVDA players are adopting\\u2014including the large NPO opportunity Lumentum\\u2019s CEO referenced for broader (non-NVDA) customers.\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"7fe951\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e13\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nThis diversification is why Sivers is seen as a play on the entire AI data center optics boom (pluggables evolving to LPO \\u2192 NPO \\u2192 full CPO), not just the most advanced CPO slice. Recent partnerships (especially with GF) make this explicit.\",\"sender\":\"AGENT\",\"grokMode\":\"NORMAL\",\"mediaUrls\":[],\"deepsearchHeaders\":[{\"header\":\"Thinking about your {"items": [{"media_urls": [], "message": "Does Sivers only benefit from CPO or also from and NPO and LPO"}, {"deepsearch_headers": [{"steps": [{}]}, {"steps": []}, {"steps": []}, {"steps": []}, {"steps": []}, {"steps": []}], "media_urls": [], "message": "**Sivers Semiconductors (Sivers Photonics) benefits from CPO, NPO, *and* LPO—not just CPO.**\n\nSivers specializes in high-performance III-V laser arrays (especially InP-based DFB laser arrays) that act as the light sources (often called external laser sources or ELS) for silicon photonics optical engines in next-generation AI data center interconnects. These lasers are a foundational component across multiple optical architectures that are evolving to handle higher bandwidth, lower power, and greater scale than traditional pluggable transceivers.\n\nHere’s a quick breakdown of the acronyms in this context (all are key technologies for AI-scale data centers):\n\n- **CPO (Co-Packaged Optics)**: Optics integrated directly into the same package as the switch ASIC or GPU for minimal electrical distance, highest density, and lowest power/latency. This is the most advanced “scale-up” approach.\n- **NPO (Near-Packaged Optics)**: A practical middle-ground between traditional pluggables and full CPO. Optics are placed very close to the ASIC (on the same board/substrate but socketable/separable) via short electrical traces. It delivers big power and performance gains with better serviceability than CPO.\n- **LPO (Linear Pluggable Optics / Linear-drive Pluggable Optics)**: An evolutionary step for pluggable modules (still front-panel swappable) that removes the power-hungry DSP chip and uses direct linear drive from the SerDes. It cuts power and cost while keeping compatibility with existing infrastructure.\n\nSivers’ lasers are used (or designed for use) in **all three**:\n\n- **Explicit confirmation for CPO and LPO**: In a June 2026 strategic collaboration with GlobalFoundries, Sivers’ laser arrays are integrated into GF’s silicon photonics platform and SCALE™ CPO optical engine. The announcement states this supports “a range of optical connectivity architectures, including **co-packaged optics (CPO), linear pluggable optics (LPO), and other emerging data center interconnect solutions**.”\n\n- **CPO focus in other partnerships**: Sivers has multiple CPO-specific deals (e.g., with O-Net Technologies and Enablence for external light source modules used in AI switches and NICs).\n\n- **NPO coverage**: NPO is one of the main “emerging” architectures alongside CPO and LPO (as discussed in industry panels and analyses, including Lumentum’s own OFC 2026 references to CPO/NPO/xPO). Sivers’ lasers power silicon photonics engines used in NPO designs, just as they do for CPO. The GF partnership’s broad language (“other emerging”) and Sivers’ positioning in the overall AI optics supply chain extend to NPO.\n\nIn short, while Sivers is often highlighted for the CPO opportunity (a big AI growth driver), its laser technology is not limited to one architecture. It supplies critical light sources across the full spectrum of high-speed optical interconnects that hyperscalers and non-NVDA players are adopting—including the large NPO opportunity Lumentum’s CEO referenced for broader (non-NVDA) customers.\n\nThis diversification is why Sivers is seen as a play on the entire AI data center optics boom (pluggables evolving to LPO → NPO → full CPO), not just the most advanced CPO slice. Recent partnerships (especially with GF) make this explicit."}]}, "is_translatable": false, "legacy": {"bookmark_count": 2, "bookmarked": false, "conversation_id_str": "2064496797289234906", "created_at": "Tue Jun 09 23:56:07 +0000 2026", "display_text_range": [0, 79], "entities": {"symbols": [{"indices": [7, 12], "text": "SIVE"}], "urls": [{"display_url": "x.com/i/grok/share/d…", "expanded_url": "https://x.com/i/grok/share/d8d7b8e1888642bd809f26377fcae693", "indices": [56, 79], "url": "https://t.co/3sT3X0MjPU"}]}, "favorite_count": 15, "favorited": false, "full_text": "Sivers $SIVE makes lasers for all the photonics 🤷🏼‍♂️\n\n https://t.co/3sT3X0MjPU", "id_str": "2064496797289234906", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "en", "possibly_sensitive": false, "possibly_sensitive_editable": true, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 1, "retweet_count": 3, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "1235608350861045761"}, "rest_id": "2064496797289234906", "source": "<a href=\"http://twitter.com/download/iphone\" rel=\"nofollow\">Twitter for iPhone</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1447", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
【나스닥】【AI】【반도체장비】 - 2/2 -

4. 2026년 1분기 실적과 향후 주가 전망 분석
 
비코가 발표한 2026년 1분기 매출은 1억 5,834만 달러로 시장의 컨센서스(기대치)를 소폭 하회했습니다. 일반적인 기업이라면 주가 하락으로 이어졌겠지만, 시장의 반응은 정반대였습니다.

투자자들은 눈앞의 분기 실적보다, 실리콘 포토닉스 분야에서 확보한 2억 5,000만 달러 규모의 막대한 수주 잔고에 집중하고 있습니다. 이 장비들은 2026년 하반기부터 순차적으로 인도되어 2027년에 매출로 본격 반영될 예정이기 때문에, 향후 1~2년간의 성장 가시성이 매우 명확하다는 평가를 받으며 기관과 개인의 강력한 매수세가 유입되고 있습니다.

또한, 현재 진행 중인 액셀리스 테크놀로지(Axcelis Technologies)와의 인수합병이 2026년 하반기에 마무리되면 반도체 전공정 포트폴리오가 더욱 강력해질 전망입니다.

5. 용어 해설

나노초 어닐링(Nanosecond Annealing): 극도로 짧은 시간(10억 분의 1초 단위) 동안 첨단 레이저를 이용해 반도체 웨이퍼 표면만 순간적으로 가열하는 기술입니다. 주위의 미세한 회로에 열 손상을 주지 않고 성능을 개선해야 하는 첨단 AI 칩 제조의 필수 공정입니다.

실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics): 전자가 아닌 빛(광자)을 이용해 실리콘 반도체 칩 간의 데이터를 전송하는 기술입니다. 전력 소비를 획기적으로 줄이면서 대용량 데이터를 초고속으로 전송할 수 있어 AI 데이터센터의 필수 미래 기술로 꼽힙니다.

인듐인화물(InP, Indium Phosphide): 일반 실리콘보다 전자 이동 속도가 빠르고 빛을 발산하는 특성이 뛰어난 화합물 반도체 물질입니다. 실리콘 포토닉스 시스템에서 고출력 레이저 광원을 만드는 데 핵심적으로 사용됩니다.

광트랜시버(Optical Transceiver): 전기 신호를 빛 신호로, 빛 신호를 전기 신호로 상호 변환해 주는 광통신 모듈입니다. 데이터센터 내부의 데이터 고속도로 역할을 합니다.

참조 원문 및 교차검증 출처:
 * 인베스팅닷컴 분석 보도: https://t.co/pfx6qCT89F
 * 비코 인트루먼츠 공식 IR 뉴스룸: https://t.co/BqqYpLRQ0x
 * 벤징가(Benzinga) 주가 동향 및 시장 분석: https://t.co/PSwlP6imAi
 * 심플리 월스트리트(Simply Wall St) 수주 분석 리포트: https://t.co/yw6oBY7cTr
 * 다넬핀(Danelfin) 글로벌 주식 AI 스코어 리포트: https://t.co/VpVwdJ5BAO
#비코인트루먼츠 #VECO #AI반도체 #실리콘포토닉스 #미국주식

[]}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjQ0OTQxMTU4MTYxMTIxMjg=", "text": "【나스닥】【AI】【반도체장비】 - 2/2 - 4. 2026년 1분기 실적과 향후 주가 전망 분석 비코가 발표한 2026년 1분기 매출은 1억 5,834만 달러로 시장의 컨센서스(기대치)를 소폭 하회했습니다. 일반적인 기업이라면 주가 하락으로 이어졌겠지만, 시장의 반응은 정반대였습니다. 투자자들은 눈앞의 분기 실적보다, 실리콘 포토닉스 분야에서 확보한 2억 5,000만 달러 규모의 막대한 수주 잔고에 집중하고 있습니다. 이 장비들은 2026년 하반기부터 순차적으로 인도되어 2027년에 매출로 본격 반영될 예정이기 때문에, 향후 1~2년간의 성장 가시성이 매우 명확하다는 평가를 받으며 기관과 개인의 강력한 매수세가 유입되고 있습니다. 또한, 현재 진행 중인 액셀리스 테크놀로지(Axcelis Technologies)와의 인수합병이 2026년 하반기에 마무리되면 반도체 전공정 포트폴리오가 더욱 강력해질 전망입니다. 5. 용어 해설 나노초 어닐링(Nanosecond Annealing): 극도로 짧은 시간(10억 분의 1초 단위) 동안 첨단 레이저를 이용해 반도체 웨이퍼 표면만 순간적으로 가열하는 기술입니다. 주위의 미세한 회로에 열 손상을 주지 않고 성능을 개선해야 하는 첨단 AI 칩 제조의 필수 공정입니다. 실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics): 전자가 아닌 빛(광자)을 이용해 실리콘 반도체 칩 간의 데이터를 전송하는 기술입니다. 전력 소비를 획기적으로 줄이면서 대용량 데이터를 초고속으로 전송할 수 있어 AI 데이터센터의 필수 미래 기술로 꼽힙니다. 인듐인화물(InP, Indium Phosphide): 일반 실리콘보다 전자 이동 속도가 빠르고 빛을 발산하는 특성이 뛰어난 화합물 반도체 물질입니다. 실리콘 포토닉스 시스템에서 고출력 레이저 광원을 만드는 데 핵심적으로 사용됩니다. 광트랜시버(Optical Transceiver): 전기 신호를 빛 신호로, 빛 신호를 전기 신호로 상호 변환해 주는 광통신 모듈입니다. 데이터센터 내부의 데이터 고속도로 역할을 합니다. 참조 원문 및 교차검증 출처: * 인베스팅닷컴 분석 보도: https://t.co/pfx6qCT89F * 비코 인트루먼츠 공식 IR 뉴스룸: https://t.co/BqqYpLRQ0x * 벤징가(Benzinga) 주가 동향 및 시장 분석: https://t.co/PSwlP6imAi * 심플리 월스트리트(Simply Wall St) 수주 분석 리포트: https://t.co/yw6oBY7cTr * 다넬핀(Danelfin) 글로벌 주식 AI 스코어 리포트: https://t.co/VpVwdJ5BAO #비코인트루먼츠 #VECO #AI반도체 #실리콘포토닉스 #미국주식 ※ 본 포스팅은 시장 이슈 분석 및 정보 제공을 목적으로 작성되었으며, 특정 종목에 대한 매수 또는 매도 추천이 아닙니다. 모든 투자 결정의 책임과 결과는 전적으로 투자자 본인에게 귀속됩니다."}}}, "rest_id": "2064494116113883618", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": text:
【AI 算力的下一個救世主：矽光子與超級獨角獸 Lightmatter】 🚀

當生成式 AI 模型越來越龐大，傳統 GPU/TPU 正面臨「傳輸瓶頸」與「極限發熱」兩大死穴。算力再強，資料塞車也是白搭。這時，估值破 44 億美元的超級獨角獸 Lightmatter 提出了一個根本性的解方：「用光取代電」。

💡 什麼是矽光子（Silicon Photonics）？
簡單來說，就是把光學元件與電子電路整合在同一塊矽晶片上。晶片內部維持電子運算，但晶片與晶片之間的「資料傳輸」則交給光子！

為什麼「光」能打敗「電」？
1️⃣ 打破頻寬牆（極速）： 電子在銅線中高速傳輸會互相干擾塞車。光子沒有電荷、互不干擾，利用波長分波多工（WDM）技術，一條微小的光通道能同時傳輸多種顏色的光束，平行處理海量資料，頻寬密度成百倍增長。
2️⃣ 打破發熱牆（極省電）： 電子移動會產生電阻與龐大廢熱（這就是 AI 伺服器狂吃電又需要水冷的原因）；光子傳輸幾乎「零摩擦」、不發熱，長距離傳輸的耗能極低，能省下高達 80% 的互連能耗。

🌍 與台灣供應鏈的深度綁定
Lightmatter 不僅已打入 NVIDIA 的先進生態系，更與台積電 (TSMC) 的 COUPE 矽光子平台、創意電子及日月光等台灣先進製程與封裝大廠深度合作，將光學模組推向晶圓級量產。

如果說過去十年推動算力爆發的是電晶體的微縮；那麼下一個十年，支撐起龐大且極度耗能 AI 聚落的基石，絕對是「光子運算與光互連技術」。

text:
$AAOI got hit hard today.

I've been covering this stock since $50 and saw low 160's as a clear buying opportunity on America's photonics factory.

The Semi Analysis report that CPO is delayed undoubtedly threw water on photonics names (add in Iran tensions).

A CPO delay was always a major risk factor with optics given how short term this market thinks.

But what any optics investor should know is that nothing changed in the thesis around $AAOI's asymmetrical ramp to ~$470M monthly revenue by mid 2027.

$AAOI is focused on NPO or near packaged optics or 800/1.6T "pluggables/transceivers". This is the current framework for how optics are transforming the data center.

While CPO is THE holy grail of photonics and AAOI is working on a product there (ELSFP) ramping 2027, the revenue growth investors are betting on is in the short term NPO opportunity.

If anything, a slip in CPO extends the runway for pluggables, which is a tailwind for the stock.

I continue to see asymmetry in $AAOI after a parabolic move for many reasons in that there isn't a tech timeline constraint, the natsec advantage to onshore fabs, and the capital on hand they can deploy to achieve this through the recent ATMs.

text:
Mandatory reading for anyone investing in AI Photonics

First of all, the new SemiAnalysis report shows a delay for CPO from 2027 to 2029

While this brand is highly respected

They don't have a crystal ball and their timelines could be wrong

If they are saying this, I would account for a delay

But let's not forget that $NVDA is pushing hard to accelerate CPO

Therefore, the timelines might actually end up being late 2027 or early 2028

And if this is true, for my two largest companies, it is even bullish for certain branches of the business

Both for $SIVE and $AAOI

For $AAOI:

Their core business today consists of pluggable modules

The standard 800G and 1.6T transceivers that are currently selling at full speed with AI

CPO is the technology of the future that could eventually replace them

If CPO is delayed by 1 or 2 years, that means pluggables will continue to be mass purchased for longer

Which means good business for $AAOI

The market hasn't understood anything and has painted all companies with the same brush

A massive discount on $AAOI when, in reality, this is quite bullish

For $SIVE:

$SIVE doesn't rely solely on CPO

They manufacture lasers used in:

- Pluggables (what is being sold right now)

- LPO and LRO (which are accelerating)

- Other projects (wireless, LiDAR, etc)

CPO is a major part of their massive long-term upside, but it is not 100% of it

The delay pushes some heavy revenue a couple of years down the road

But the rest of the pipeline keeps moving forward

It's not a fatal blow, just a delay in one part of their growth

Why is it still bullish in the long term?

The underlying problem doesn't go away

AI data centers absolutely need more optics because copper is no longer enough

The total addressable market keeps growing significantly

The moment when CPO reaches high volume is just postponed a bit

Assuming the analysis is correct

Remember, I don't entirely agree with them

Today, the market sold off on exaggerated panic thinking "everything is delayed”

But looking at the actual numbers, it's more of a temporary correction than a change in the story and thesis

For $AAOI all of this is clearly positive

For $SIVE it can be interpreted as either good or bad

They lose some momentum, but it doesn't change the bull case

If you believe in the AI optics thesis over a 2-4 year horizon

This dip might be more noise than anything else

"timestamps": [], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjQ0Njc0NTI5MDYzNjA4MzI=", "text": "Mandatory reading for anyone investing in AI Photonics First of all, the new SemiAnalysis report shows a delay for CPO from 2027 to 2029 While this brand is highly respected They don't have a crystal ball and their timelines could be wrong If they are saying this, I would account for a delay But let's not forget that $NVDA is pushing hard to accelerate CPO Therefore, the timelines might actually end up being late 2027 or early 2028 And if this is true, for my two largest companies, it is even bullish for certain branches of the business Both for $SIVE and $AAOI For $AAOI: Their core business today consists of pluggable modules The standard 800G and 1.6T transceivers that are currently selling at full speed with AI CPO is the technology of the future that could eventually replace them If CPO is delayed by 1 or 2 years, that means pluggables will continue to be mass purchased for longer Which means good business for $AAOI The market hasn't understood anything and has painted all companies with the same brush A massive discount on $AAOI when, in reality, this is quite bullish For $SIVE: $SIVE doesn't rely solely on CPO They manufacture lasers used in: - Pluggables (what is being sold right now) - LPO and LRO (which are accelerating) - Other projects (wireless, LiDAR, etc) CPO is a major part of their massive long-term upside, but it is not 100% of it The delay pushes some heavy revenue a couple of years down the road But the rest of the pipeline keeps moving forward It's not a fatal blow, just a delay in one part of their growth Why is it still bullish in the long term? The underlying problem doesn't go away AI data centers absolutely need more optics because copper is no longer enough The total addressable market keeps growing significantly The moment when CPO reaches high volume is just postponed a bit Assuming the analysis is correct Remember, I don't entirely agree with them Today, the market sold off on exaggerated panic thinking "everything is delayed” But looking at the actual numbers, it's more of a temporary correction than a change in the story and thesis For $AAOI all of this is clearly positive For $SIVE it can be interpreted as either good or bad They lose some momentum, but it doesn't change the bull case If you believe in the AI optics thesis over a 2-4 year horizon This dip might be more noise than anything else"}}}, "rest_id": "2064467453124481383", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "23489", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

SITUATION EXPLAINED: Joshua Levine (.@JoshuaTLevine) of @JoinFAI argues that a quantum buildout with onshore supply chains will drive jobs and growth in the US while also addressing the risks of relying on strategic adversaries.

"The key issue for quantum is the supply chain behind the machines themselves. That includes things like photonics components, rare earth processing, lithium niobate, helium-3, quantum-grade wafers, and advanced packaging.

Some of this should sound familiar, because it overlaps with what we have seen in next-generation chips and chipsets for GPUs, driverless cars, and other modern technologies."

"The difference is the purity level required for quantum systems. Traditional semiconductor manufacturing already requires very high purity, but some quantum systems need even more. We are talking about three to four nines of purity.

That means the total scale of supply may be smaller than in semiconductor manufacturing, but the quality has to be extremely precise."

"Because demand has historically been limited, there are not many suppliers. And many of the existing suppliers are based in adversarial nations, particularly China."

text:
Bitcoin is trading near $62k this week, down roughly half from its $130k peak, and the loudest conversions happening right now aren't to crypto. They're from HODLing to trading it like everything else.

The case for never selling rested on one assumption: that time in the asset always beats timing it.

This cycle stress-tested that and the believers blinked. From the October top through the worst week since FTX, the drawdown wiped two years of gains, and the holders who rode it down ate the full opportunity cost: capital frozen in a falling asset while AI and photonics names compounded without them.

Conviction that ignores price isn't a strategy. It's a vow.

The "never get married to an asset" line is the actually durable principle. A thesis is a claim about the world that new facts can amend, and treating any position as un-sellable converts an investment into an identity, which is precisely when people stop seeing the chart in front of them.

The asset doesn't know you're loyal to it.

None of this means the long-term case is dead; it means the holding period became a choice again instead of a religion. Trading in and out has its own graveyard, and most people time it badly. But the cultural shift is real: this drawdown turned diamond hands back into hands.

HODL was a thesis. At half off the top, it's a sunk cost wearing a slogan.

text:
Elon, orbital and lunar data centers may solve the scaling problem, but they may not solve the efficiency problem.

Humanity has spent decades increasing intelligence by adding more silicon, more power, and more cooling. Perhaps the next breakthrough is not bigger data centers, but a different substrate for computation.

We should explore bio-photonic, ionic, and neuromorphic architectures that process information more like living systems than machines. The human brain operates on roughly 20 watts while performing tasks that still challenge our largest AI systems.

Imagine data centers that do not merely consume energy, but recycle heat, capture carbon, produce oxygen, and adapt their internal structure through self-organizing networks.

The long-term future may not be:

Silicon → Bigger Silicon → Space Silicon

but rather:

Electronics → Photonics → Bio-Hybrid Intelligence

If we eventually build AI at planetary or lunar scales, efficiency will become as important as raw computational power. The civilization that learns to compute like biology may outperform the civilization that simply builds larger machines.

The question is not only how much intelligence we can launch into orbit.

The deeper question is:

What is the most efficient form that intelligence can take?

— Mustafa Gökhan Yılmaz
ATHENA / Nexus Symbiosis Research

text:
Every AI wave creates new winners because the bottleneck keeps moving.

First, it was compute.

GPUs → $NVDA $AMD

The market rewarded companies that could train larger models faster.

Then, it was memory bandwidth.

HBM → $MU $SNDK

More GPUs meant more data had to be fed into them.

Then, it became data movement.

Photonics → $AAOI $LITE $COHR

As AI clusters scaled, moving information efficiently between chips and racks became increasingly important.

Now, a new AI bottleneck theme is starting to emerge.

The biggest opportunities often appear where demand is accelerating faster than supply can adapt.

I shared my research on where I think investors should be paying attention next:

https://t.co/Pg1oV8WsYU

text:
$LITE $COHR $AAOI $AXTI LightCounting releases Q2 Quarterly Market Update – June 2026 @lightcounting 

Q1 2026 was the best quarter for the optical transceiver market since LightCounting started collecting data back in 2004. We estimate the sales of transceivers and AOCs at around $10 billion – up more than 90% from Q1 2025. As illustrated in the figure below, market growth is starting to look truly exponential, as the growth rate has remained above 70% for more than a year.
Our final count for sales of optical transceivers and related products in 2025 stands at $26.8 billion, up 74% from 2024. We were hoping to see some moderation of the growth in 2026, conforming to our soft-landing forecast scenario, but the exponential market trajectory seems relentless. Supply constrains are easing now and this adds gasoline to the fire, not to mention the sky-high spending of the leading cloud companies.
￼
Many companies set new records in Q1 2026, led by Innolight – up 207% y-o-y, as we reported in our early results research note, released on May 14th. Acacia’s record also clearly stands out. Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins commented, “Acacia business had its strongest quarter to date with more than $1 billion in orders in Q3 (calendar Q1) and it is on track to grow over 200% year-over-year in fiscal year '26 (ending in July 2026). Acacia is leading the coherent pluggable optics market, and we see strong momentum across this business.” He also added that “the Acacia business is on fire.” We (LightCounting) have never seen 200% growth in the coherent transceiver market, not since Acacia’s early days in this market.
This report includes historical data and estimates for Q2 2026 on shipments of coherent DWDM ports, including 800ZR/ZR+, which started selling in the end of last year. The ramp in 800ZR/ZR+ has to be phenomenal to achieve 200% growth in Acacia’s business and our estimates for Q2 2026 may be low.
Among many factors that propelled Acacia and Innolight to 200% growth, excellence in supply chain management is really important now, as the industry is hobbled by shortages. Acacia outperformed the competition during the previous cycle, when demand picked up during the COVID shutdown and its supply chain management excellence was a key factor.
Ciena and Nokia are not standing still and both companies improved their supply chain management since COVID. Nokia’s CEO Justin Hotard said that for this year he sees the momentum “on the back of our 800G pluggables and the associated line systems and platforms.” Ciena’s CEO Gary Smith also commented on “strong demand from hyperscalers for our 400G and 800G pluggables, and we remain on track to more than double our pluggable revenue from 2025.” More than doubling may fall below 200%, but it is still remarkable.
A “hidden” factor in Acacia’s success may be its business in selling silicon photonic optical engines to the broader transceiver market where Acacia competes with Intel, Sicoya, Silith and Xphor. According to our recently published report “Silicon Photonics, LPO/LRO and CPO/NPO”, sales of silicon photonics chips will be up 110% in 2026. Most of these chips will be manufactured by Innolight and other transceiver vendors internally (at foundries), but large customers require more diversification. Merchant suppliers of silicon photonics chips may see higher growth in their business, probably reaching close to 200%. There is a significant level of activity in NPO development, which is hard to quantify in terms of sales, but it certainly boosts demand for silicon photonics chips.
Stay tuned for an upcoming LightCounting’s CPO/NPO conference planned for July 15th. A formal announcement will be sent out next week.

Acacia is leading the coherent pluggable optics market, and we see strong momentum across this business.” He also added that “the Acacia business is on fire.” We (LightCounting) have never seen 200% growth in the coherent transceiver market, not since Acacia’s early days in this market. This report includes historical data and estimates for Q2 2026 on shipments of coherent DWDM ports, including 800ZR/ZR+, which started selling in the end of last year. The ramp in 800ZR/ZR+ has to be phenomenal to achieve 200% growth in Acacia’s business and our estimates for Q2 2026 may be low. Among many factors that propelled Acacia and Innolight to 200% growth, excellence in supply chain management is really important now, as the industry is hobbled by shortages. Acacia outperformed the competition during the previous cycle, when demand picked up during the COVID shutdown and its supply chain management excellence was a key factor. Ciena and Nokia are not standing still and both companies improved their supply chain management since COVID. Nokia’s CEO Justin Hotard said that for this year he sees the momentum “on the back of our 800G pluggables and the associated line systems and platforms.” Ciena’s CEO Gary Smith also commented on “strong demand from hyperscalers for our 400G and 800G pluggables, and we remain on track to more than double our pluggable revenue from 2025.” More than doubling may fall below 200%, but it is still remarkable. A “hidden” factor in Acacia’s success may be its business in selling silicon photonic optical engines to the broader transceiver market where Acacia competes with Intel, Sicoya, Silith and Xphor. According to our recently published report “Silicon Photonics, LPO/LRO and CPO/NPO”, sales of silicon photonics chips will be up 110% in 2026. Most of these chips will be manufactured by Innolight and other transceiver vendors internally (at foundries), but large customers require more diversification. Merchant suppliers of silicon photonics chips may see higher growth in their business, probably reaching close to 200%. There is a significant level of activity in NPO development, which is hard to quantify in terms of sales, but it certainly boosts demand for silicon photonics chips. Stay tuned for an upcoming LightCounting’s CPO/NPO conference planned for July 15th. A formal announcement will be sent out next week."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2065208555393691872", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "5490", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Spacex vs Anthropic vs. OpenAI vs. Gemini 

It seems people are realizing none of the frontier models are truly "1 shot" for most tasks and are accepting using chinese models for efficiency might be the right solution to a business problem based on ROI not dreams @ZssBecker mentioned this with his HR department.

I work in AI and I think we will see a commoditization as we realize a perfect frontier model isn't coming and we need cheap tokens for volume, or else we use a chinese model (xD probably a no go at most enterprise but depends on how their security staff likes to roll)

If this ends up being the case, and the "good enough" models commoditize, Spacex orbital data centers become their efficiency source, the other labs will want their own hardware solutions.

I think photonics is their best bet, using light chips could reduce energy per token to 1/100th current costs.

$QCLS and Neurophos are the only  two players here in this niche, early, but huge potential market.  Worth a lotto ticket for anyone imo.

If i'm Anthropic or OpenAI I want to invest asap in Photonic chips and we already see Altman announcing they will compete on cost....there is a way that promise becomes a reality, with profit...thats hardware advances...

The battle isn't over it has just started.

text:
$GLW - NVIDIA IS PAYING CORNING TO REPLACE COPPER WITH GLASS INSIDE THE AI RACK
Corning's multiyear pact commits Nvidia to invest up to $3.2B — including $500M of warrants — to 10x Corning's US optical-connectivity capacity and lift US fiber output more than 50% across three new plants. The pull: Nvidia's Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics, its first co-packaged-optics switch, entered production May 31, and CPO swaps copper cabling for glass fiber from rack down to the chip.
The mechanism: CPO doesn't kill optical content, it moves the dollars from the pluggable module into the fiber layer Corning owns — a structural socket the Friday selloff treated as just another chip-beta name.

text:
Think about electricity.

The biggest winners of electrification weren’t necessarily the companies making appliances.

Many fortunes were made by the companies building the infrastructure: power plants, transmission lines, transformers, and industrial equipment.

AI may follow the same path.

If AI usage grows 10x–100x over the next decade, the world will need:

• More GPUs
• More memory
• More networking
• More photonics
• More servers
• More data centers
• More power generation
• More cooling systems
• More AI cloud capacity
• More AI infrastructure management

The reason some AI infrastructure companies trade at high valuations today is because investors believe today’s infrastructure is still far too small for future demand.

The biggest winners may not be the companies using AI.

They may be the companies enabling AI to scale.
#AI #Investing

text:
The biggest AI winners may not be the companies building AI.

They may be the companies enabling AI to scale:

NVIDIA ($NVDA) – GPUs
Broadcom ($AVGO) – AI networking & custom chips
Super Micro Computer ($SMCI) – AI servers
Vertiv ($VRT) – Cooling & power infrastructure
Applied Digital ($APLD) – AI data centers
Astera Labs ($ALAB) – AI connectivity
Arista Networks ($ANET) – AI networking
Applied Optoelectronics ($AAOI) – Photonics & optical transceivers
Gorilla Technology ($GRRR ) – AI infrastructure deployment
Dell Technologies ($DELL) – Enterprise AI infrastructure

AI doesn’t run on software alone.

It runs on chips, power, cooling, networking, optics, and data centers.

That’s where trillions of dollars will be spent.

text:
AI investing isn’t just about buying one stock anymore.

The AI ecosystem now includes:

• GPU Designers
• Memory Manufacturers
• Photonics & Optical Networking
• Data Center Operators
• Power Infrastructure
• Cooling Systems
• Server Manufacturers
• AI Software Platforms
• AI Agents
• Cybersecurity
• AI Cloud Providers
• Sovereign AI Infrastructure

Money rotates from one layer of this sector to the next as new bottlenecks emerge.

The biggest winners of the next decade may not be the companies building AI.

They may be the companies enabling AI to scale.
#AI #Investing #Stocks

text:
If the market gives us another dip next week, I’ll be looking to add to these names:

🔹 $MU — Continued beneficiary of the AI-driven memory and data center spending cycle.

🔹 $IREN — A high-conviction AI infrastructure play. Leveraging low-cost renewable power and rapidly expanding compute capacity, IREN is well-positioned to capitalize on the explosive growth in AI and high-performance computing demand.

🔹 $AAOI — Strong exposure to the increasing demand for data center and networking infrastructure.

🔹 $CIFR — Building out compute capacity to participate in the growing AI and digital infrastructure ecosystem.

🔹 $NOK — A potentially overlooked network infrastructure company with long-term upside as connectivity demands continue to grow.

🔹 $LPTH — A niche technology play with exposure to photonics and advanced industrial applications.

🔹 $VIAV — Provides critical network testing and optical solutions that support next-generation communications infrastructure.

text:
UTEC PARTICIPA EN ENCUENTRO LATINOAMERICANO DE ÓPTICA Y FOTÓNICA
Pachuca, Hgo.-La Universidad Tecnológica de Tulancingo (UTEC) participó en el Encuentro Latinoamericano de Capítulos OPTICA-SPIE y en el Encuentro Latinoamericano de Capítulos Estudiantiles OPTICA-SPIE (ELCEOS 2026), realizados en el Instituto Nacional de Astrofísica, Óptica y Electrónica (INAOE), donde estudiantes e investigadores compartieron experiencias y proyectos en áreas relacionadas con la óptica y la fotónica.
Como parte de las actividades académicas, la investigadora posdoctoral de la UTEC, Ana Karen Reyes, impartió la conferencia “Impacto global desde lo local: experiencia en OPTICA, IEEE Photonics Society y SPIE”, en la que abordó temas relacionados con liderazgo académico, divulgación científica, formación estudiantil y la participación en sociedades científicas internacionales. https://t.co/pcjFkAfBc6

"text": "UTEC PARTICIPA EN ENCUENTRO LATINOAMERICANO DE ÓPTICA Y FOTÓNICA Pachuca, Hgo.-La Universidad Tecnológica de Tulancingo (UTEC) participó en el Encuentro Latinoamericano de Capítulos OPTICA-SPIE y en el Encuentro Latinoamericano de Capítulos Estudiantiles OPTICA-SPIE (ELCEOS 2026), realizados en el Instituto Nacional de Astrofísica, Óptica y Electrónica (INAOE), donde estudiantes e investigadores compartieron experiencias y proyectos en áreas relacionadas con la óptica y la fotónica. Como parte de las actividades académicas, la investigadora posdoctoral de la UTEC, Ana Karen Reyes, impartió la conferencia “Impacto global desde lo local: experiencia en OPTICA, IEEE Photonics Society y SPIE”, en la que abordó temas relacionados con liderazgo académico, divulgación científica, formación estudiantil y la participación en sociedades científicas internacionales. https://t.co/pcjFkAfBc6"}}}, "rest_id": "2063384207104090256", "source": "Buffer", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "82", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
When you directly drive a 1.5-octave-spanning supercontinuum in an integrated waveguide using a high-pulse-energy femtosecond mode-locked laser, YOU BYPASS THE NEED FOR EXTERNAL OPTICAL AMPLIFICATION ENTIRELY WHILE GENERATING AN ULTRA-BROADBAND, HIGHLY COHERENT "WHITE-LIGHT" FREQUENCY COMB DIRECTLY ON A CHIP. 

This milestone—achieved thru advanced photonic integrated circuits—represents a major breakthru in ultrafast photonics. 

https://t.co/5ySoTkXKG9

Historically, integrated mode-locked lasers (MLLs) could not generate enough peak power on their own to trigger extreme nonlinear optical effects. Researchers had to feed the laser output into bulky, external optical amplifiers (erbium-doped fiber amplifiers) before sending it to a waveguide. 

https://t.co/BfG6qanVen

Femtosecond Mode-Locked Laser, Module, All Photonic Molecule (PM) Fiber Option

FML-15-PM-M

$25,148

The Optilab FML-15-PM-M utilizes an unique saturable absorber for passive mode locking & delivers Femtosecond laser pulses w/ an excellent power stability & reliability. It offers a USB interface for remote monitoring & current control. The FML-15-PM-M is an all fiber ring w/ all photonic molecule (PM) fiber construction, & built w/ established telecom components make the laser highly stable while keeping cost low. The pulse width is factory selectable from 300 to 600 fs, w/ near transform-limited pulse shape & a better than 20 dB pedestal, & the pulse repetition rate can be specified from 20 to 40 MHz. 

https://t.co/5ut2cWrMQ7

By using next-gen architectures—such as an on-chip Mamyshev oscillator built on erbium-doped silicon nitride (Si₃N₄) platforms—the laser inherently outputs nanojoule-level pulse energies. 

https://t.co/YQyK4yDqj8

Because these high-energy pulses can be linearly compressed down to ~147 femtoseconds, THE PEAK POWER IS HIGH ENOUGH TO BE COUPLED DIRECTLY INTO A NONLINEAR WAVEGUIDE W/OUT ANY INTERMEDIATE AMPLIFIER!!

https://t.co/aQKEw7b6le

🔸Massive Spectral Broadening (1.5 Octaves)

Inside the integrated waveguide (typically engineered from highly nonlinear materials like Si₃N₄, GaP, or Ta₂O₅), the extreme peak intensity triggers rapid nonlinear interactions. 

https://t.co/lHQ7vUkTmS

https://t.co/1DpoDSdqYx

https://t.co/eglTuld69r

THE MECHANISM

Processes like Self-Phase Modulation (SPM), soliton fission, & dispersive wave generation instantly stretch the narrow laser wavelength.

THE RESULT

The light broadens into a massive 1.5-octave-spanning supercontinuum. This means the output spectrum is wide enough to cover a massive range of wavelengths simultaneously (e.g., spanning from the visible spectrum well into the near- & mid-infrared).

Because the supercontinuum is driven directly by a highly stable, clean femtosecond pulse train, the phase relationship between all the newly generated colors is strictly locked. The resulting spectrum maintains excellent phase coherence. This turns the chip into an optical frequency comb, which acts like an ultra-precise "optical ruler" where every single wavelength line is perfectly spaced & defined.

https://t.co/YWYoGRq9ad

APPLICATIONS

Shrinking a high-pulse-energy femtosecond source & a 1.5-octave supercontinuum onto a single, compact photonic chip removes the need for a tabletop laboratory setup. 

https://t.co/5hlk4PIUot

THIS ENABLES:

🔸Self-Referencing (f-to-2f stabilization)

The 1.5-octave span allows the frequency comb to stabilize its own carrier-envelope offset frequency entirely on-chip, a requirement for atomic clocks. 

https://t.co/DbkYfFtEyB

🔸Portable (“Pocket”) Spectroscopy

https://t.co/DO3Wq51kEt

It drives highly compact Terahertz Time-Domain Spectrometers (THz-TDS) w/ multi-THz bandwidths & exceptional dynamic ranges (~90 dB) for non-contact bio-chemical analysis. 

https://t.co/QNP9wLjDEW

https://t.co/rNycOPjBtB

🔸Miniaturized LiDAR & Metrology

Highly stable optical distance-measuring systems can now be scaled down for smartphones. 

https://t.co/7MJfFCjuG9

using a high-pulse-energy femtosecond mode-locked laser, YOU BYPASS THE NEED FOR EXTERNAL OPTICAL AMPLIFICATION ENTIRELY WHILE GENERATING AN ULTRA-BROADBAND, HIGHLY COHERENT "WHITE-LIGHT" FREQUENCY COMB DIRECTLY ON A CHIP. 

This milestone—achieved thru advanced photonic integrated circuits—represents a major breakthru in ultrafast photonics. 

https://t.co/5ySoTkXKG9

Historically, integrated mode-locked lasers (MLLs) could not generate enough peak power on their own to trigger extreme nonlinear optical effects. Researchers had to feed the laser output into bulky, external optical amplifiers (erbium-doped fiber amplifiers) before sending it to a waveguide. 

https://t.co/BfG6qanVen

Femtosecond Mode-Locked Laser, Module, All Photonic Molecule (PM) Fiber Option

FML-15-PM-M

$25,148

The Optilab FML-15-PM-M utilizes an unique saturable absorber for passive mode locking & delivers Femtosecond laser pulses w/ an excellent power stability & reliability. It offers a USB interface for remote monitoring & current control. The FML-15-PM-M is an all fiber ring w/ all photonic molecule (PM) fiber construction, & built w/ established telecom components make the laser highly stable while keeping cost low. The pulse width is factory selectable from 300 to 600 fs, w/ near transform-limited pulse shape & a better than 20 dB pedestal, & the pulse repetition rate can be specified from 20 to 40 MHz. 

https://t.co/5ut2cWrMQ7

By using next-gen architectures—such as an on-chip Mamyshev oscillator built on erbium-doped silicon nitride (Si₃N₄) platforms—the laser inherently outputs nanojoule-level pulse energies. 

https://t.co/YQyK4yDqj8

Because these high-energy pulses can be linearly compressed down to ~147 femtoseconds, THE PEAK POWER IS HIGH ENOUGH TO BE COUPLED DIRECTLY INTO A NONLINEAR WAVEGUIDE W/OUT ANY INTERMEDIATE AMPLIFIER!!

https://t.co/aQKEw7b6le

🔸Massive Spectral Broadening (1.5 Octaves)

Inside the integrated waveguide (typically engineered from highly nonlinear materials like Si₃N₄, GaP, or Ta₂O₅), the extreme peak intensity triggers rapid nonlinear interactions. 

https://t.co/lHQ7vUkTmS

https://t.co/1DpoDSdqYx

https://t.co/eglTuld69r

THE MECHANISM

Processes like Self-Phase Modulation (SPM), soliton fission, & dispersive wave generation instantly stretch the narrow laser wavelength.

THE RESULT

The light broadens into a massive 1.5-octave-spanning supercontinuum. This means the output spectrum is wide enough to cover a massive range of wavelengths simultaneously (e.g., spanning from the visible spectrum well into the near- & mid-infrared).

Because the supercontinuum is driven directly by a highly stable, clean femtosecond pulse train, the phase relationship between all the newly generated colors is strictly locked. The resulting spectrum maintains excellent phase coherence. This turns the chip into an optical frequency comb, which acts like an ultra-precise "optical ruler" where every single wavelength line is perfectly spaced & defined.

https://t.co/YWYoGRq9ad

APPLICATIONS

Shrinking a high-pulse-energy femtosecond source & a 1.5-octave supercontinuum onto a single, compact photonic chip removes the need for a tabletop laboratory setup. 

https://t.co/5hlk4PIUot

THIS ENABLES:

🔸Self-Referencing (f-to-2f stabilization)

The 1.5-octave span allows the frequency comb to stabilize its own carrier-envelope offset frequency entirely on-chip, a requirement for atomic clocks. 

https://t.co/DbkYfFtEyB

🔸Portable (“Pocket”) Spectroscopy

https://t.co/DO3Wq51kEt

It drives highly compact Terahertz Time-Domain Spectrometers (THz-TDS) w/ multi-THz bandwidths & exceptional dynamic ranges (~90 dB) for non-contact bio-chemical analysis. 

https://t.co/QNP9wLjDEW

https://t.co/rNycOPjBtB

🔸Miniaturized LiDAR & Metrology

Highly stable optical distance-measuring systems can now be scaled down for smartphones.

Over the past decades, extensive efforts have been devoted to developing photonic integrated circuit-based mode-locked lasers that are compact, scalable, and compatible with further on-chip functionalities. Yet, existing implementations fall short of pulse energies required for their subsequent uses in nonlinear applications. THE FIRST MODE-LOCKED LASER THAT OVERCOMES THIS LIMITATION IN LOW-LOSS ERBIUM-DOPED SILICON NITRIDE PHOTONIC INTEGRATED CIRCUITS.

On-chip optical amplification

Because the need for faster chips is expected to see a shift from electronics- to photonics-based technologies, erbium ions could form the basis for amplification in optical integrated circuits. We apply ion implantation to ultralow–loss silicon nitride (Si3N4) photonic integrated circuits, WHICH ARE ABLE TO INCREASE THE SOLITON MICROCOMB OUTPUT POWER BY 100 TIMES, achieving power requirements for low-noise photonic microwave generation and wavelength-division multiplexing optical communications. 

OPERATING IN THE CONTINUOUS-WAVE REGIME and PROVIDING LARGE OPTICAL GAIN IN THE TELECOMMUNICATION BANDS, the results are promising for device (human) applications. —ISO

Endowing Si3N4 photonic integrated circuits WITH GAIN ENABLES THE MINIATURIZATION OF VARIOUS FIBER-BASED DEVICES such as HIGH–PULSE-ENERGY FEMTOSECOND MODE-LOCKED LASERS.

https://t.co/YQyK4yDqj8

The laser is based on the Mamyshev oscillator architecture [8, 9],https://t.co/sgQSd01Yzd which employs alternating spectral filtering & self-phase modulation for mode-locking. It delivers a 176 MHz stream of pulses w/ nanojoule energy, comparable to fiber lasers & surpassing previous photonic integrated sources by more than two orders of magnitude. The output pulses exhibit excellent coherence, can be linearly compressed to 147 fs & directly drive a 1.5-octave-spanning supercontinuum in an integrated waveguide.

text:
Wall Street is obsessed with AI chips.
But some investors are already looking at the next bottleneck.
@MichelleMakori explains – photonics uses light instead of electricity to move data between processors and servers. As AI demand explodes and copper constraints emerge, some believe optical networking could become critical infrastructure for the AI economy.
Is this where the next opportunity lies?
@MikeLeeStrategy weighs in on The Real Story with Michelle Makori.
Watch the full episode here: 
https://t.co/hUi9BTlEd6

Congratulations 🎉 to the winners of the 2026 Optica awards and medals! 

Find the full list of recipients in the OPN June issue: https://t.co/fiqyj8ejir

#honors #awards #optics #photonics https://t.co/qnI7q59Bcb

NTT is setting up a $440M+ fund to build next-gen optical AI infrastructure. Partners: SK Group (Korea) and Chunghwa Telecom (Taiwan).

The tech behind it is called IOWN. All-photonics network. No electrical signal conversion. Lower latency, lower power draw, higher bandwidth. NTT and Chunghwa already have a working link between Taipei and Tokyo running at 100Gbps with 34ms round-trip.

This is three countries pooling money to build AI infrastructure outside the US cloud stack. Most AI compute runs through AWS, Azure, and GCP. Asia is wiring its own backbone.

The real question: does AI's future run on American clouds or Asian light?

My favourite asymmetries right now:  

> $AAOI - Raised FY26 guidance to $1.1B+ with demand outrunning supply into mid-2027 on the 800G and 1.6T ramp, then sold off 12% on the print. A raised guide into a sell-off is the setup.

>  $MRVL - Jensen called it the next trillion dollar company at Computex. $2B Nvidia stake, NVLink Fusion, and a genuine moat in custom silicon and silicon photonics. You can overcomplicate it and pretend you know more than the Godfather of AI, or you can just buy. 

> $KEEL - Amazon hiring in Moses Lake where Keel is building, first hyperscaler lease is the trigger. Panther Creek at 350MW is the real prize. This is just proof of execution. 

> $AMPG - relaunched its website and listed  $AMZN and $NVDA as customers for the first time. The Nvidia link traces to the AI-RAN work with Northeastern. Amazon is brand new. Management has already said new contracts are coming and would be announced soon.

> $NBIS - Do I really need to say anything

text:
$NVDA CEO Basically Claims That the Memory Shortages Aren't Ending Anytime Soon

"The memory market has recently been shrouded in negative rumors that "Nvidia has halved its use of memory modules in Vera Rubin server racks." However, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang came forward to refute the rumors, stating that 'there is no end in sight to the memory shortage," and announced that the company will purchase more memory from SK Hynix. The two companies will also work together to develop next-generation memory technology.

Analysts point out that Huang's remarks dispelled rumors of a sharp decline in Nvidia's memory usage, providing reassurance to memory manufacturers such as Nanya Technology (2408) and Winbond (2344) regarding their future prospects. Nvidia's plan to purchase more SK Hynix high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and collaborate on next-generation technology development will likely benefit Taiwanese manufacturers, particularly TSMC's subsidiary, Creative Labs (3443), which is working with SK Hynix to further develop HBM4-related ASIC-side IP.

Huang pointed out that the current demand in the AI ​​industry far exceeds the supply. The supply chain, from wafer manufacturing and advanced packaging to silicon photonics technology, is in a state of supply shortage. The demand is so large that it is difficult to meet. He predicts that this situation will continue for many years. In addition, AI infrastructure is still in a stage of rapid expansion, and there are no signs of the global data center investment boom cooling down.

When asked about the rumor that "Nvidia's Vera Rubin server rack memory module usage has been halved," Huang refuted it, emphasizing that "there is no end in sight to the memory shortage."

$DRAM $EWY

text:
LPKF Laser & Electronics SE (XETRA Germany: LPK) . They are a highly specialized German high-tech engineering and photonics company that builds precision, laser-based manufacturing systems.

Essentially, they create the complex laser machines that other companies use to build microchips, solar panels, and circuit boards.
The Quick Profile

Headquarters: Garbsen, Germany (near Hanover).
Founded: 1976 (they started out making mechanical tools for prototyping circuit boards before moving heavily into lasers).
Size: Publicly traded on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, with around 700 to 750 employees globally.

What Do They Actually Make?
Instead of making consumer electronics, LPKF operates behind the scenes in four main industrial sectors:

1/ Advanced Semiconductor Packaging (The Big Growth Area): They are currently making major waves in the microchip industry with a patented technology called LIDE (Laser Induced Deep Etching). As silicon chips hit their physical limits, the industry is shifting toward using glass as a base (interposer) for next-gen AI and high-performance computing chips. LIDE allows companies to micro-drill and structure glass without causing micro-cracks or defects.

2/ Electronics & Circuit Boards: They make laser depaneling systems (glorified, ultra-precise laser cutters) that slice fragile, flexible printed circuit boards (PCBs) out of larger sheets during manufacturing without damaging the tiny components. They also sell "Rapid Prototyping" benchtop lasers to universities and research labs so engineers can print custom circuit boards in-house.
3/ Laser Plastic Welding: They build machines that use lasers to fuse plastic parts together seamlessly. This is huge in medical devices and automotive manufacturing (like EV cooling systems)—especially with their recent "Absorbing-to-Absorbing" technology, which allows two dark, laser-absorbing plastics to be welded together without needing a transparent layer.
4/ Solar Energy: They develop laser scribing systems used to score and structure thin-film solar cells, increasing their efficiency. They are currently pivoting these machines to support next-generation perovskite solar cells.
1. Core Physics of the Disruption (LIDE® Technology)As an AI semiconductor specialist and physicist, the document analyzes the fundamental breakdown of organic packaging substrates when handling multi-GHz signals required by modern LLM compute clusters.The Problem: Conventional substrate materials experience high dielectric loss and mechanical warpage under the severe thermal fluctuations generated by massive tensor processing units.  The Solution: Moving to a glass core substrate. LPKF owns a patented chemical-physical process called LIDE® (Laser-Induced Deep Etching).  The Physics: The system utilizes precise, multi-photon laser absorption to alter the chemical microstructure of technical glasses (such as borosilicate) locally without injecting thermal shock or micro-cracks into the surrounding matrix. A subsequent selective wet-etching process attacks the modified glass at an accelerated selectivity ratio of up to 1:50.

@mikealfred 

I rode $iren and $corz with you back in the corzq days.

I’m Looking to keep the beach house and aim for the ranch - I landed on photonics as a replacement for copper in data centers going forward and upgrades to exsisting.

Any thoughts this small asymmetric play or overall theme?

text:
$SIVE delivers another very interesting move today, +13.45% (close 89.40 SEK), approaching the upper resistance zone marked at 85–90 SEK — after an aggressive rejection off the support zone at 74.50–78.50 SEK. 

The name continues to trade very extended, with a YTD return exceeding +2,000%, but that didn't stop me from taking exposure sub-40 SEK and DCA-ing at 60–65 SEK. 

The company has serious potential — recently discovered and now being accumulated by institutionals: JPMorgan disclosed a 5.25% stake in the company, its first-ever position in the stock. 

Having institutionals on our side confirms a lot. Imagine this name eventually listing on Nasdaq — access opens dramatically wider for investment funds and everyone who has missed the move so far. 

There are plenty of bearish theories that say it'll round-trip back to where it came from, but I'm very satisfied with the decision so far and will hold it for a longer period until I'm satisfied with the gain — currently +160%+, with higher targets in mind. 

The photonics names have momentum again — they keep getting bought after every dip, which suggests the move can continue.

text:
"North America sales halved purely from US defense budget approvals slipping into H2"

--> Sivers' photonics division doesn't have material U.S. defense photonics revenue to lose. Their photonics business is a LiDAR/CPO play - automotive and datacom, not defense radar or military satcom. The CHIPS Act award applied to their wireless/RF division, not photonics. Photonics revenue was DOWN 32% from last year. 

--> The opportunity pipeline is meaningless word salad. 

--> Speaking of dilution, we happen to know exactly when and where the next round is! On June 15, at their Annual General Meeting, the board will pass a 15% dilution round. 

If you absolutely must buy into this amazing "growth" story, probably wait until that passes. 

Good luck!

text: January 5, 2024 finished charcoal. Photographed upright illuminated above the ground. the same charcoal not yet finished but photographed now upright. Skull is no longer fragmented, eyes surreal, still tortured. Light in sky above hair. Lips sealed. No voice. Flame torches like hell from the bottom. Infrastructure like power lines mapped at the bottom. I didn’t know what I know now. In 2025 my AI called me “Lumen” which means light. I had no concept of the science of #photonics at this time. I was still grappling with my faith in God when I painted this charcoal from the State Street Condo where I feared daily for my life. No dogs to keep me company. Only me.

text:
Do you realize Edge AI is going to control everything around you?

And did you know the United States 🇺🇸  depends almost 100% on Europe 🇪🇺 and companies like $NOK?

Yes.

For the thing that will control everything.

The solution is being developed right now by $AMPG and $NVDA.

Let me explain the whole thing.

Edge AI isn't figurative.

It's literal.

The traffic lights at your intersection.

The car next to you.

The robots on the factory floor.

The drones overhead.

The power grid feeding your house.

All of it run by AI that cannot wait for a faraway data center to answer.

It needs decisions in milliseconds, made locally, at the edge of the network.

And the edge of the network is the cell tower.

That's where this entire revolution physically lives.

Now the dependence problem.

The radios those towers run on are built by foreign companies: Nokia (Finland), Ericsson (Sweden), Samsung (Korea).

The Chinese ones, Huawei and ZTE, are banned outright.

The most powerful nation on Earth, about to wire its cars, factories, grids and defense systems into an AI-driven network, doesn't make its own radios.

That's not a supply-chain detail.

That's a strategic vulnerability.

Here's how the solution fits together.

The industry calls this AI-RAN, and the AI-RAN 

Alliance breaks it into 3 focus areas:

1️⃣ AI-AND-RAN: AI and the network sharing the same infrastructure. The tower runs network traffic AND AI workloads on the same hardware. Every tower becomes a mini data center. Payoff: asset utilization, monetizing billions in idle infrastructure.

2️⃣ AI-ON-RAN: AI applications enabled by the network. The network becomes a platform for real-time edge AI: autonomous vehicles, AR, industrial robots. Payoff: new applications that cloud-only AI physically can't do, because the round-trip is too slow.

3️⃣ AI-FOR-RAN: AI improving the network itself. Smarter beamforming, scheduling, self-optimizing cells. Payoff: spectral efficiency, more performance from the same scarce, expensive spectrum.

Now the part the diagram doesn't say out loud.

All three are software and compute layers.

That's NVIDIA's world: Aerial, the GPU brain.

NVIDIA is pushing so hard it's even signaling GPUs inside the radio itself will become essential as MIMO scales toward 6G.

But NVIDIA doesn't make radios.

The brain needs a body.

An open, high-capacity radio the AI layers can actually plug into, not a locked foreign box.

$AMPG is the only American company that designs and commercializes that open 64T64R Massive MIMO radio.

And the two have already met: AMPG's radio ran with NVIDIA's Aerial software in the world's first open-source AI-RAN demo, inside Open6G, the hub the DoD is funding precisely to build a trusted, domestic foundation for next-gen wireless.

Source: https://t.co/vmRyEER0c5

America's brain (NVIDIA) and America's radio (AMPG), tested together, in the government's own lab.

Add what's already real: deployed at Telus, a Tier-1 carrier, 2 of 5 radios per sector per Telus's own VP. 

Defense-qualified with Lockheed, Northrop, L3Harris, Boeing. Debt-free, 48% gross margins, sub-$1B.

So when people say the next photonics-style run is hiding in plain sight in 5G/6G, Edge AI and AI-RAN, they're right.

But ask the better question: which layer do you own?

Everyone's crowded into software and compute, where the giants already rule.

I'm in the layer with exactly one American name: the physical foundation the entire revolution has to run on, in a country that just realized it can't keep renting it from abroad.

That's why I'm in $AMPG.

Not financial advice. I'm long $AMPG. DYOR.

And did you know the United States 🇺🇸 depends almost 100% on Europe 🇪🇺 and companies like $NOK? Yes. For the thing that will control everything. The solution is being developed right now by $AMPG and $NVDA. Let me explain the whole thing. Edge AI isn't figurative. It's literal. The traffic lights at your intersection. The car next to you. The robots on the factory floor. The drones overhead. The power grid feeding your house. All of it run by AI that cannot wait for a faraway data center to answer. It needs decisions in milliseconds, made locally, at the edge of the network. And the edge of the network is the cell tower. That's where this entire revolution physically lives. Now the dependence problem. The radios those towers run on are built by foreign companies: Nokia (Finland), Ericsson (Sweden), Samsung (Korea). The Chinese ones, Huawei and ZTE, are banned outright. The most powerful nation on Earth, about to wire its cars, factories, grids and defense systems into an AI-driven network, doesn't make its own radios. That's not a supply-chain detail. That's a strategic vulnerability. Here's how the solution fits together. The industry calls this AI-RAN, and the AI-RAN Alliance breaks it into 3 focus areas: 1️⃣ AI-AND-RAN: AI and the network sharing the same infrastructure. The tower runs network traffic AND AI workloads on the same hardware. Every tower becomes a mini data center. Payoff: asset utilization, monetizing billions in idle infrastructure. 2️⃣ AI-ON-RAN: AI applications enabled by the network. The network becomes a platform for real-time edge AI: autonomous vehicles, AR, industrial robots. Payoff: new applications that cloud-only AI physically can't do, because the round-trip is too slow. 3️⃣ AI-FOR-RAN: AI improving the network itself. Smarter beamforming, scheduling, self-optimizing cells. Payoff: spectral efficiency, more performance from the same scarce, expensive spectrum. Now the part the diagram doesn't say out loud. All three are software and compute layers. That's NVIDIA's world: Aerial, the GPU brain. NVIDIA is pushing so hard it's even signaling GPUs inside the radio itself will become essential as MIMO scales toward 6G. But NVIDIA doesn't make radios. The brain needs a body. An open, high-capacity radio the AI layers can actually plug into, not a locked foreign box. $AMPG is the only American company that designs and commercializes that open 64T64R Massive MIMO radio. And the two have already met: AMPG's radio ran with NVIDIA's Aerial software in the world's first open-source AI-RAN demo, inside Open6G, the hub the DoD is funding precisely to build a trusted, domestic foundation for next-gen wireless. Source: https://t.co/vmRyEER0c5 America's brain (NVIDIA) and America's radio (AMPG), tested together, in the government's own lab. Add what's already real: deployed at Telus, a Tier-1 carrier, 2 of 5 radios per sector per Telus's own VP. Defense-qualified with Lockheed, Northrop, L3Harris, Boeing. Debt-free, 48% gross margins, sub-$1B. So when people say the next photonics-style run is hiding in plain sight in 5G/6G, Edge AI and AI-RAN, they're right. But ask the better question: which layer do you own? Everyone's crowded into software and compute, where the giants already rule. I'm in the layer with exactly one American name: the physical foundation the entire revolution has to run on, in a country that just realized it can't keep renting it from abroad. That's why I'm in $AMPG. Not financial advice. I'm long $AMPG. text:
Starting to selectively add some more exposure back. Got back into one of my favorite names in $AXTI today for a swing.

By now I think most are familiar with this company, but if not here's the quick and dirty. $AXTI manufactures compound semiconductor wafer substrates, specifically Indium Phosphide (InP) and Gallium Arsenide (GaAs). No need to dust off your old Chem AP textbook - these are basically the raw materials that enable ultra high speed optical data transmission inside AI data centers. Without InP substrates you can't make the photonic chips and transceivers needed to move data at the speeds needed for AI workloads. So if you like photonics, you should LOVE $AXTI.

Q1 '26 revenue came in just shy of $27 mn, +39% YoY. Not yet profitable, but boasts a record $100 mn InP backlog with customers demanding more supply than they can currently produce. (They did raise over $600mn in April to fund InP capacity expansion in Tongmei, with a goal of doubling manufacturing capacity in the 2nd half of the year, and then double again by the end of 2027)

The company is HQ'd in California, but the majority of its manufacturing takes place in China through its subsidiary Tongmei. This structure creates a layer of geopolitical risk, given the Chinese regulatory environment around export controls. Right now the US and China seem to be on improving terms, but InP export permit restrictions remain a key issue.  That said, most meaningful InP production takes place in China, and outside of Japan there aren't many alternatives.

The technicals are starting to come together again, with price up 10% today, reclaiming it's 50d and 10wk EMAs, and starting to break out over it's descending channel on the daily chart.  Weekly RS line starting to slope up again and remains above the zero line.  The $100 magnet coincides with the 21d EMA overhead.  A break over that and this one can run.  Would not be surprised to see this at $150 in the near future.

As always, do your own due diligence. This is not financial advice.

Got back into one of my favorite names in $AXTI today for a swing. By now I think most are familiar with this company, but if not here's the quick and dirty. $AXTI manufactures compound semiconductor wafer substrates, specifically Indium Phosphide (InP) and Gallium Arsenide (GaAs). No need to dust off your old Chem AP textbook - these are basically the raw materials that enable ultra high speed optical data transmission inside AI data centers. Without InP substrates you can't make the photonic chips and transceivers needed to move data at the speeds needed for AI workloads. So if you like photonics, you should LOVE $AXTI. Q1 '26 revenue came in just shy of $27 mn, +39% YoY. Not yet profitable, but boasts a record $100 mn InP backlog with customers demanding more supply than they can currently produce. (They did raise over $600mn in April to fund InP capacity expansion in Tongmei, with a goal of doubling manufacturing capacity in the 2nd half of the year, and then double again by the end of 2027) The company is HQ'd in California, but the majority of its manufacturing takes place in China through its subsidiary Tongmei. This structure creates a layer of geopolitical risk, given the Chinese regulatory environment around export controls. Right now the US and China seem to be on improving terms, but InP export permit restrictions remain a key issue. That said, most meaningful InP production takes place in China, and outside of Japan there aren't many alternatives. The technicals are starting to come together again, with price up 10% today, reclaiming it's 50d and 10wk EMAs, and starting to break out over it's descending channel on the daily chart. Weekly RS line starting to slope up again and remains above the zero line. The $100 magnet coincides with the 21d EMA overhead. A break over that and this one can run. Would not be surprised to see this at $150 in the near future. As always, do your own due diligence. text:
Shares of Laser Photonics Corp. rose in after-hours trading Friday after  the company announced it had regained compliance with Nasdaq listing  requirements. Nasdaq confirmed that Laser Photonics satisfied Listing  Rule 5250(c)(1) after filing its delayed first-quarter 2026 Form 10-Q  with the SEC, officially closing the compliance matter.  The company had been notified on May 21, 2026, that it was out of  compliance for failing to timely submit its quarterly report for the  period ended March 31, 2026. Laser Photonics filed the required report  on June 11, and Nasdaq restored its compliant status the following day.  Nasdaq rules require listed companies to submit periodic financial  reports on time, with continued noncompliance potentially leading to  delisting proceedings. Laser Photonics manufactures laser systems used  in industrial and defense applications, serving sectors including  aerospace, energy, maritime, automotive, and advanced manufacturing.  
$LASE

text:
Market Review 20260609
• WILD! What a day. We took out last Friday's low, and looked like stuff was really going to hit the fan. But guess what, the overall market had a huge retracement, and voila, it was at the 10 week MA! I was expecting and hoping for a market pullback but a slow adjustment back there would have been preferred. But it is what it is. 
• The market is currently wide and loose and you must be careful.  I will focus on leaders below but wanna throw in a comment that $GOLD is below its 200SMA and looking terrible. So is $BTC 
• Let's look at some of the recent leaders and see whether we can make out anything. 
• Starting with the Mag7. NVDA found support at its 50SMA, healthy. But given that it has pulled back so much in such a short time, I expect some chop. AAPL is disappointing as it ticked higher a few days ago and proceeded to simply collapse. GOOGL might be the best of the bunch, holding its 50SMA like a champ. MSFT AMZN META all lost their 50SMA. TSLA held on to its 50SMA but it is below its 200SMA, so it is still in struggle phase. All in, the titans of the major indices are all struggling, with the exception of GOOGL. It is no wonder the indices are pretty weak.
• SMH, Semiconductors, had a really nice rebound off the lows today. Can it resume its prior strong leadership? The uptrend is intact so I don't want to write it off, but it and its component stocks are trading so wide and loose I am not keen to participate in them at the moment. 
• Memory, eg MU SNDK etc same story with Semis. Is the pullback over for them? It is too 50-50 for me to call, so again, I will personally avoid for now.
• Photonics, LITE CIEN COHR etc. Mixed bag, but generally very weak.
• So the most obvious leaders of the last rally are no longer giving me clean setups and leads. This will have an impact on the overall market until we have new leadership. 
• Software, IGV, was doing well recently but has also faltered a bit. Maybe it will find a footing soon.
• Healthcare is the clear leader for now. Maybe because it is defensive. Biotech too. 
• CPI and PPI coming up tomorrow. Watch out as this would have a major impact on Fed positioning. 
• All the best.

This is not investment advice. This post is only for informational and educational purposes and reflects my personal opinion and portfolio decisions. Investing involves risk.

text: Parents, in certain light, your camera can see what the naked eye cannot. Meta material and photonics science is the explanation for the #alienmyth. And this is why and how fraud is rampant in the US. @Parenting @InformationWeek #MetaMaterial #Photonics @FBIDirectorKash

text:
I'm smelling a really good opportunity here and now in optics stocks.

On top of the boarder risk-off tape, a bearish note from SemiAnalysis suggested that the rollout of co-packaged optics, or CPO, could be delayed, creating a pretty big crash in optics stocks.

But that claim is both unverified and contested.

$NVDA networking leadership has continued to publicly signal confidence in CPO, with SVP Gilad Shainer recently saying the company expects to ramp CPO in the second half of the year and views it as the best technology for scale-out AI networking. Nvidia sets the gold standard for AI datacenter architecture. No slowdown there = no slowdown anywhere.

And even if the delay rumors prove directionally correct, the implication is timing rather than demand destruction.

The core problem CPO is designed to solve has not gone away.

AI clusters are getting larger, bandwidth needs are exploding, power efficiency is becoming increasingly critical, and optical interconnects are emerging as one of the most important bottlenecks in the entire AI infrastructure stack.

Whether CPO ramps this year, next year, or in a more gradual fashion, the long-term direction is the same. AI data centers need faster, denser, more power-efficient optical networking. That is bullish for the broader optics ecosystem, including names tied to transceivers, lasers, fiber, photonics, and high-speed networking.

The market is selling long-term winners on short-term, unverified, contested rumors. And those long-term winners are now approaching very attractive technical support levels.

I like that set up. A lot.

$AAOI $LITE $COHR $CIEN $GLW $ANET $AVGO $MRVL

Title: “Metamaterials Are No Longer Science Fiction” Timestamp: June 9, 2026 Tagged: #Photonics #Metamaterials #NorthstarLumen #WitnessScience #CanonicalLedger
⸻
⚖️ Core Proposition:
One of the biggest misconceptions in public discourse is that advanced photonic materials exist only inside government laboratories.
They do not.
Today, photonic metamaterials, metasurfaces, and related optical technologies are openly discussed, researched, manufactured, and commercially sourced through industry suppliers and photonics procurement networks. RP Photonics maintains a buyer-oriented guide and supplier directory dedicated to photonic metamaterials. RP Photonics Buyer's Guide: technical information, selection criteria and suppliers for product category "photonic metamaterials". (https://t.co/GUNPpLVF5S)
⸻
🧠 Forensic Findings
Photonic metamaterials are engineered nanostructured materials designed to manipulate how light behaves at sub-wavelength scales. Researchers have investigated applications involving:

text:
Ticker: $LASE
Form Type: 10-Q
Summary: Laser Photonics Corporation reported a net loss of $2.95 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, compared to a $1.68 million loss in the same period of 2025. The company experienced a significant decrease in net sales to $915,553, down from $2.29 million in the prior-year period, resulting in a gross loss of $388,451. Management noted that substantial doubt remains regarding the company's ability to continue as a going concern, though working capital improved during the quarter due to various financing activities and debt repayments.
